meanings.definition: cause to become detached or separated; take off
examples: detach the skin from the chicken before you eat it

meanings.definition: separate (a small unit) from a larger, especially for a special assignment

examples: His retina detached and he had to be rushed into surgery

meanings.definition: showing lack of emotional involvement; - J.S.Perelman
examples: adopted a degage pose on the arm of the easy chair
she may be detached or even unfeeling but at least she's not hypocritically effusive

meanings.definition: being or feeling set or kept apart from others; ; - Sherwood Anderson
examples: she felt detached from the group
could not remain the isolated figure he had been
thought of herself as alone and separated from the others
on one side of the island was a hugh rock, almost detached
the separated spacecraft will return to their home bases

meanings.definition: used of buildings; standing apart from others

examples: the detached shutter fell on him
he pulled his arm free and ran
